tests.test_body:
define i8 @iv_used_in_exit_with_math(i8 noundef %g) {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i8 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %s = shl nuw i8 1, %iv
  %a = and i8 %s, %g
  %c = icmp eq i8 %a, 0
  br i1 %c, label %loop.latch, label %return

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i8 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i8 %iv.next, 4
  br i1 %ec, label %return, label %loop.header

return:                                           ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop.header
  %res.iv1 = phi i8 [ 32, %loop.latch ], [ %iv, %loop.header ]
  %res.iv2 = phi i8 [ 0, %loop.latch ], [ %iv, %loop.header ]
  %res = add i8 %res.iv1, %res.iv2
  ret i8 %res
}

issue.title: wrong code at -O{s,2} with "-mllvm -force-vector-width=1 -mllvm -force-vector-interleave=2" on x86_64-linux-gnu
issue.author: zhendongsu

comments.author: MacDue
comments.body:
This seems to be an issue with early-exit vectorization not handling a return value depending on the induction variable correctly. It looks like it just passes through the vectorized induction `vp<%4>`:

```
<x1> vector loop: {
  vector.body:
    EMIT vp<%3> = CANONICAL-INDUCTION ir<0>, vp<%index.next>
    vp<%4> = SCALAR-STEPS vp<%3>, ir<1>, vp<%0>
    CLONE ir<%0> = shl nuw ir<1>, vp<%4>
    CLONE ir<%1> = and ir<%0>, ir<%g>
    CLONE ir<%tobool.not> = icmp eq ir<%1>, ir<0>
    EMIT vp<%index.next> = add nuw vp<%3>, vp<%1>
    EMIT vp<%5> = not ir<%tobool.not>
    EMIT vp<%6> = any-of vp<%5>
    EMIT vp<%7> = icmp eq vp<%index.next>, vp<%2>
    EMIT vp<%8> = or vp<%6>, vp<%7>
    EMIT branch-on-cond vp<%8>
  No successors
}
Successor(s): middle.split

middle.split:
  EMIT branch-on-cond vp<%6>
Successor(s): vector.early.exit, middle.block

middle.block:
  EMIT vp<%cmp.n> = icmp eq ir<32>, vp<%2>
  EMIT branch-on-cond vp<%cmp.n>
Successor(s): ir-bb<return>, scalar.ph

vector.early.exit:
Successor(s): ir-bb<return>

ir-bb<return>:
  IR   %storemerge.lcssa = phi i32 [ 32, %for.inc ], [ %storemerge2, %for.body ] (extra operands: ir<32> from middle.block, vp<%4> from vector.early.exit)
  IR   %retval.0 = phi i32 [ 0, %for.inc ], [ %storemerge2, %for.body ] (extra operands: ir<0> from middle.block, vp<%4> from vector.early.exit)
  IR   store i32 %storemerge.lcssa, ptr @a, align 4, !tbaa !7
No successors
```

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Don't think this is related to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/153946, it is specific to VF = 1. Will look into a fix

history.commit_subject: [VPlan] Add ExtractLane VPInst to extract across multiple parts. (#148817)
history.commit_message:
[VPlan] Add ExtractLane VPInst to extract across multiple parts. (#148817)

This patch adds a new ExtractLane VPInstruction which extracts across
multiple parts using a wide index, to be used in combination with
FirstActiveLane.

The patch updates early-exit codegen to use it instead ExtractElement,
which is only per-part. With this change, interleaving should work
correctly with early-exit loops.

The patch removes the restrictions added in 6f43754e9 (#145877), but
does not yet automatically select interleave counts > 1 for early-exit
loops.

I'll share a patch as follow-up. The cost of extracting a lane adds
non-trivial overhead in the exit block, so that should be considered
when picking the interleave count.

PR: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/148817

tests.test_body:
target triple = "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

; Function Attrs: vscale_range(1,16)
define i64 @same_exit_block_pre_inc_use1() #0 {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 513
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %loop ], [ 67, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

attributes #0 = { vscale_range(1,16) "target-features"="+sve" }

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @multi_exiting_to_different_exits_live_in_exit_values() {
entry:
  %src = alloca [128 x i32], align 4
  call void @init_mem(ptr %src)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %inc, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.src = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %src, i64 %iv
  %l = load i32, ptr %gep.src, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp eq i32 %l, 10
  br i1 %c.1, label %e1, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %inc = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %c.2 = icmp eq i64 %inc, 128
  br i1 %c.2, label %e2, label %loop.header, !llvm.loop !0

e1:                                               ; preds = %loop.header
  %p1 = phi i64 [ 0, %loop.header ]
  ret i64 %p1

e2:                                               ; preds = %loop.latch
  %p2 = phi i64 [ 1, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %p2
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.interleave.count", i32 4}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @same_exit_block_pre_inc_use1_reverse() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ], [ 1023, %entry ]
  %gep.p1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %iv
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep.p1, align 1
  %gep.p2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %iv
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep.p2, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, -1
  %exitcond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop.end, label %loop

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop ], [ 1024, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @diff_exit_block_post_inc_use1() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.p1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %iv
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep.p1, align 1
  %gep.p2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %iv
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep.p2, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.early.exit

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.early.exit:                                  ; preds = %loop
  %retval1 = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop ]
  ret i64 %retval1

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc
  %retval2 = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval2
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @same_exit_block_post_inc_use() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.p1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %iv
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep.p1, align 1
  %gep.p2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %iv
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep.p2, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop ], [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @diff_exit_block_pre_inc_use1() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.p1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %iv
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep.p1, align 1
  %gep.p2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %iv
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep.p2, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.early.exit

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.early.exit:                                  ; preds = %loop
  %retval1 = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop ]
  ret i64 %retval1

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc
  %retval2 = phi i64 [ 67, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval2
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define ptr @same_exit_block_pre_inc_use1_ivptr() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  %ptrend = getelementptr i8, ptr %p1, i64 1024
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %ptr = phi ptr [ %ptr.next, %loop.inc ], [ %p1, %entry ]
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %ptr, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, 72
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %ptr.next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne ptr %ptr.next, %ptrend
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi ptr [ %ptr, %loop ], [ %ptrend, %loop.inc ]
  ret ptr %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @multi_exiting_to_different_exits_live_in_exit_values() {
entry:
  %src = alloca [128 x i32], align 4
  call void @init_mem(ptr %src)
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %inc, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.src = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %src, i64 %iv
  %l = load i32, ptr %gep.src, align 4
  %c.1 = icmp eq i32 %l, 10
  br i1 %c.1, label %e1, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %inc = add nuw i64 %iv, 1
  %c.2 = icmp eq i64 %inc, 128
  br i1 %c.2, label %e2, label %loop.header

e1:                                               ; preds = %loop.header
  %p1 = phi i64 [ 0, %loop.header ]
  ret i64 %p1

e2:                                               ; preds = %loop.latch
  %p2 = phi i64 [ 1, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %p2
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i8 @same_exit_block_reverse_use_loaded_value() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ], [ 1023, %entry ]
  %gep.p1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %iv
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep.p1, align 1
  %gep.p2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %iv
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep.p2, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, -1
  %exitcond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop.end, label %loop

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi i8 [ %ld1, %loop ], [ -1, %loop.inc ]
  ret i8 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i8 @same_exit_block_use_loaded_value() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.p1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %iv
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep.p1, align 1
  %gep.p2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %iv
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep.p2, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 1024
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop.end, label %loop

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi i8 [ %ld1, %loop ], [ -1, %loop.inc ]
  ret i8 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @same_exit_block_pre_inc_use1() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %gep.p1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %iv
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %gep.p1, align 1
  %gep.p2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %iv
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %gep.p2, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop ], [ 67, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

; Function Attrs: nofree nosync
define i8 @test_early_exit_max_tc_less_than_16(ptr dereferenceable(16) %A) #0 {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %p.src = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %l = load i8, ptr %p.src, align 1
  %c = icmp eq i8 %l, 0
  br i1 %c, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 16
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop.header
  %res = phi i8 [ 0, %loop.header ], [ 1, %loop.latch ]
  ret i8 %res
}

attributes #0 = { nofree nosync }

target datalayout = "e-m:o-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

; Function Attrs: nofree nosync
define i64 @test_early_exit_max_tc_less_than_16_with_iv_used_outside(ptr dereferenceable(16) %A) #0 {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %p.src = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %A, i64 %iv
  %l = load i8, ptr %p.src, align 1
  %c = icmp eq i8 %l, 0
  br i1 %c, label %exit, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.header
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 16
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop.header
  %res = phi i64 [ %iv, %loop.header ], [ 1, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %res
}

attributes #0 = { nofree nosync }
